item.title: Iran War Just Canceled a Better Economic Outlook for the World
item.description: Imagine an alternative reality where Donald Trump opted against attacking Iran.

item.title: TikTok Plans Bigger, Bolder Actions With Future Secured
item.description: The threat of a US ban no longer overshadows the app

item.title: EU Targets Snapchat, Porn Websites in Child Protection Crackdown
item.description: The European Union opened an investigation into Snap Inc.’s social network and escalated a probe into a number of pornography platforms as the bloc ramps up efforts to protect children online.

item.title: Iceland’s Inflation Climbs, Paving Way for More Rate Hikes
item.description: Iceland’s inflation rate rose to the highest level in a year and a half, driven by the cost of oil and putting pressure on the island’s central bank to tighten its monetary stance even further.

item.title: BOE’s Breeden Plays Down Risk of Second-Round Inflation Effects
item.description: Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden played down the threat of a wage-price feedback loop in response to the Iran War, saying a weak economy means workers and firms have less bargaining power.

item.title: Germany Sees Danger of 2026 Growth Rate Halving on Iran Crisis
item.description: German officials see a risk that the nation’s economy will grow at just half the pace they had envisaged if the Iran war drags on, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Coca-Cola Bottler Raising Euro Bonds to Buy Africa Business
item.description: Coca-Cola HBC Finance BV is raising at least €1.5 billion ($1.7 billion) of debt on Thursday to help fund an acquisition of another drinks bottling firm.

item.title: Wall Street Piles Into Cash in Hopes of a Stock Market Rebound
item.description: Traders have Trump’s April tariff reversal in mind as they weigh whether to sell.

item.title: EU Lawmakers Approve US Trade Deal After Several Delays
item.description: European Union lawmakers finally approved a trade deal with the US, clearing a key obstacle for the long-delayed agreement despite lingering uncertainty about Washington’s tariffs.

item.title: US Agency to Own 20% of Graphite Miner Syrah in Critical Minerals Push
item.description: US International Development Finance Corp. is swapping a loan made to graphite producer Syrah Resources Ltd. for equity, as Washington seeks to shore up access to critical metals and reduce dependence on China.

item.title: Germany to Consider Additional Fuel Measures, Minister Says
item.description: German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche said the coalition may introduce additional measures to help alleviate the impact of surging fuel prices after lawmakers approved an initial package.

item.title: Cathay Raises Fuel Levies Again, to Review Them Every Two Weeks
item.description: Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd. increased fuel surcharges for the second time in as many weeks, raising them by 34% across all types of tickets, as it ramped up the frequency of its fuel-levies reviews to biweekly.

item.title: Litigation Funder Fights Petronas as Royal Heirs’ $15 Billion Victory Unravels
item.description: Litigation funder Therium Capital Management Ltd. is fighting a lawsuit alleging that it played a “central role” in a cross-continent conspiracy to get a $14.9 billion arbitration payout from Malaysia.

item.title: Private Credit Flows Falter Amid Defaults, AI Disruption Fears
item.description: Private credit fund inflows in the first two month of this year dropped by more than a third as investors grew concerned about high-profile leveraged loan defaults and software disruption, according to Morningstar Direct.

item.title: Car Insurer First Central Is Said to Tap Banks for London IPO
item.description: First Central Group Ltd., the UK vehicle and home insurer, has appointed investment banks as it prepares for a potential initial public offering in London, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Tourism Is Huge in Mexico. Spending on Cultural Sites Isn’t
item.description: The government has gutted funding for archeological digs, monuments and their preservation, putting at risk the country’s enviable tourist draw.

item.title: War Hits Global Economy With US Inflation Seen by OECD at 4.2%
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is reviving the specter of inflation and hobbling the global economy just as it was showing signs of strengthening at the start of the year, the OECD said.

item.title: ‘Five Nights at Epstein’s’ Game Goes Viral at US School Campuses
item.description: Merve Lapus’s 13-year-old daughter told her dad she was horrified: This month, whenever her teacher stepped out of the room, kids in the class used their devices to play a new online game called Five Nights at Epstein’s.

item.title: UK Faces Worst Hit From War Among Major Economies, OECD Warns
item.description: Britain will suffer a bigger economic hit from the Iran war than any other Group of Seven nation this year as growth weakens, inflation accelerates and interest-rate cuts are abandoned, the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development warned.

item.title: Japan Invites Blanchard to Panel in Bid to Boost Global Outreach
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s government invited Olivier Blanchard to speak at an economic panel as she seeks to showcase her administration’s so-called “responsible” fiscal approach to a global audience even as she increases spending.

item.title: Meituan Posts Single-Digit Sales Growth During China Food Fight
item.description: Meituan reported a modest 4.1% increase in sales as the company’s expansion outside China helped offset pressure from an intensifying price war with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and JD.com Inc. at home.

item.title: Sewage Spills in England Fell 35% as Drought Curbed Overflows
item.description: Sewage spills in England fell by more than a third last year as unusually dry weather curbed storm overflows, but the country’s water industry remains far short of its pollution targets.

item.title: Norway Pivots Toward Hike After Keeping Its Rate Steady at 4%
item.description: Norway’s central bank opened the door to an interest-rate hike this year and even discussed an immediate move to tame inflation before then deciding to keep borrowing costs unchanged.

item.title: Petro’s Approval Rating Jumps Ahead of Colombia’s Election
item.description: The approval rating of Colombia’s leftist leader Gustavo Petro rose to its highest level since 2024, potentially boosting the chances of his ally Iván Cepeda in the presidential election in May.

item.title: Lula Struggles to Blunt Bolsonaro’s Rise in Tight Brazil Race
item.description: Brazil President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva is struggling to respond to Flavio Bolsonaro’s rise in polls as tensions mount within his inner circle over how to counter the surge, adding to a string of crises denting his approval and threatening his chances in the final electoral campaign of his career.

item.title: Pony AI Swings to Profit, Aims to Launch Robotaxis in 20 Cities
item.description: Pony AI Inc. delivered its first profitable quarter ever, bolstered by a windfall from an early investment, rather than its main robotaxi business.

item.title: Army Taps Private Capital to Build Data Centers on Its Bases
item.description: The US Army has announced the first group of companies that will build data centers on military bases, as the armed forces address a growing need for artificial intelligence computing power.

item.title: Milei’s Approval Rating Hits New Low as Argentina Unemployment Rises
item.description: President Javier Milei’s approval rating fell in March to the lowest level since he took office as corruption allegations ensnared his government, unemployment climbed and Argentines soured on his trade deal with the Trump administration.

item.title: ECB Is ‘Unwavering’ in Ensuring Price Stability, Guindos Says
item.description: The European Central Bank is “closely monitoring” the economic effects of the Iran war and is determined to achieve price stability, Vice President Luis de Guindos said.

item.title: Banxico Faces Hard Choice Between Cut or Hold: Decision Day Guide
item.description: Mexico’s central bank is likely to hold its benchmark interest rate steady or deliver a small cut later Thursday, with analysts unusually split between the two outcomes after last month’s decision to leave borrowing costs unchanged.
